config.exam_name: 23 Databricks COPY INTO Command

question.line: What is the primary purpose of the COPY INTO command in Databricks?

options.B: To ingest files from cloud storage or Unity Catalog volumes into Delta tables
options.D: To replace Auto Loader in all scenarios

question.line: Which of the following file formats is supported by the COPY INTO command?

options.B: CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro, Text, and Binary files

question.line: What does it mean that COPY INTO is idempotent?

options.A: It rewrites all files every time it runs.
options.B: It creates duplicate records to ensure consistency.
options.C: Running the command multiple times does not reload previously processed files.
options.D: It requires manual cleanup after every execution.
options.E: It automatically deletes the source files after loading.

question.line: Why does the lesson create a placeholder Delta table before using COPY INTO?

options.B: To create an empty table without a predefined schema so COPY INTO can infer it automatically
options.D: To convert CSV files into Parquet before loading
options.E: To create a backup of the source files

question.line: Which capability allows COPY INTO to automatically create columns and detect data types when loading data into a placeholder table?
Where does COPY INTO maintain metadata about previously processed files?
How can COPY INTO perform simple transformations while loading data?

options.A: By using Delta Live Tables only
options.B: By wrapping the source data in a SELECT statement that performs transformations
options.E: By creating a temporary view first

question.line: What happens when a new source file is added to the landing folder and COPY INTO is executed again?

options.B: Only the new file is processed while previously loaded files are skipped.
options.C: The Delta table is recreated from scratch.
options.D: Existing records are deleted before loading.
options.E: COPY INTO fails because duplicate files exist.

question.line: Which feature of COPY INTO allows loading data into a table whose schema changes over time?
According to the lesson, when should Auto Loader generally be preferred over COPY INTO?

options.A: When loading a few CSV files once a month
options.B: When processing small batch workloads with hundreds of files
options.C: When handling millions of continuously arriving files and complex directory structures
options.E: When reading data from Unity Catalog volumes only
